chunks.text: Isaac Newton, in his later years
spoke of himself in this way: " I hardly know anything about what the world is.
Few people have ever given humanity such great knowledge.
The law of gravity, the three laws of motion, the discovery of calculus.
Before Newton, no one could calculate the orbit of a planet with accuracy.
His mathematics itself rebuilt the very language of science.
Yet he never allowed himself to become proud.
" I am like a child on the seashore, picking up a pebble or two.
Before me, the vast ocean of truth lies almost untouched.
Newton's humility teaches us that however much one knows, it is never enough.
Theory may extend far, but the ocean of knowledge is deeper still.
